title: CLINUVEL will cut up to 20% of staff, relocate HQ to New York
snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

title: BeOne commits $300M to US manufacturing expansion, creating 120 new jobs
snippet: The addition of small molecule capacity at BeOne Medicines’ Hopewell site will support the company’s hematology efforts and bring total investment in the campus to more than $1 billion.

title: Lilly preps FDA run for ‘triple-G’ weight loss drug with Phase 3 data
snippet: With weight loss topping 20% at 80 weeks in the Phase 3 TRIUMPH-2 and TRIUMPH-3 trials, Eli Lilly plans to file for retatrutide’s approval in the first quarter of 2027. Previously a filing was expected later this year.

title: Roche's late stage pipeline 'so full,' CEO Schinecker not feeling pressure to add on
snippet: Roche has 19 investigational medicines that could launch by 2030—and even if not all of them make it to market, the company is “well set-up for growth into the next decade,” CEO Thomas Schinecker said on the pharma’s Q2 earnings call.

title: GSK’s $10.6B Nuvalent bet pays off early as FDA approves lung cancer drug
snippet: Jideytro is GSK’s first approved product for lung cancer. With its takeover of Nuvalent Bio last month and a deal with Hansoh Pharmaceutical in 2023, the pharma now has several other lung cancer assets in late-stage development.

title: Nordic biotech’s next act: From fragmentation to global force
snippet: In this episode of Denatured, you’ll hear from Ester Sklarsky, principal at Sound Bioventures and João Ribas, partner at Novo Holdings Seed Investments. In this episode, we explore the diverging trajectories of Nordic biotech and what it will take for the region, including Norway and Finland, to compete more cohesivel...

title: Biopharma layoffs must double in H2 for 2026 to match 2025 cuts
snippet: With fewer biopharmas making or planning workforce reductions so far year over year, layoff round size and M&A impact on headcounts will likely shape the number of employees who are ultimately on the chopping block.

title: Make or break: Commercial hires critical for success
snippet: With the stakes for successful launches high, and demand for commercial roles increasing, biopharmas must thoughtfully approach not only hiring but also retention. During a BioSpace webinar, experienced commercial leaders and a recruiter discussed the challenges employers face and how to tackle them.

title: Webinar: From Clinical to Commercial: Talent Strategies for Biopharma Growth
snippet: This webinar provides an assessment of recent job market activity across biopharma and explores how companies approaching commercial readiness or building out sales and marketing teams can navigate today’s hiring trends.

title: Summit and Akeso sharpen case for ivonescimab with new Western data
snippet: Summit Therapeutics and Akeso are answering questions raised about ivonescimab’s ability to translate to Western populations, though the partners have yet to share if the new findings are statistically significant.

title: Arrowhead puts pressure on Ionis with positive data for triglyceride lowering drug
snippet: With Phase 3 data showing what analysts called a “potentially best-in-class profile,” Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals plans to submit for FDA approval of plozasiran by the end of the year—hoping to follow to market Ionis Pharmaceuticals’ Tryngolza, which got the regulator’s nod in severe hypertriglyceridemia last month.

title: Repligen strikes $1.5B BioLife buyout to bag cell therapy product pharmas rely on
snippet: Top-selling cell therapies from BMS, J&J and Gilead depend on a BioLife product, positioning Repligen to use the takeover to increase its exposure to the modality.

title: 6 companies hiring in Cambridge now
snippet: Looking for a biopharma job in Cambridge? Roles posted on BioSpace have more than doubled compared to last year.

title: US ‘eroding’ lead in rare diseases a matter of national security: Report
snippet: A Congressional advisory panel recommends the U.S. government put supportive policies in place for rare disease drug development, such as a finalized platform designation from the FDA and the allowance of novel trial designs for small patient populations.

title: 2026 IPO stock tracker: How newly public biotechs are performing
snippet: BioSpace is launching an IPO dashboard to help the industry keep tabs on U.S. biotechs that have taken the public plunge in 2026.

title: Novartis scraps mid-stage radiopharma asset as Pluvicto soars
snippet: The discontinued asset, being trialed for breast cancer and solid tumors, had yielded disappointing data, leading to Novartis’ decision to abandon development, according to a company spokesperson.

title: Q2 earnings underway, FDA’s CRL kerfuffle, IPOs full speed ahead in H2
snippet: Second quarter earnings continue to roll with Novartis and Roche reporting this week; Eli Lilly’s AtaiBeckley buy for up to $3.8 billion offers clear “validation” for the fledgling psychedelics space; more drama unfolds at FDA as the agency goes back and forth on its policy of publishing complete response letters in r...

title: From Big Pharma to specialty: why moving closer to the patient changes everything
snippet: Working at a Big Pharma has its benefits, but there are limitations too. SERB Pharmaceuticals’ Thomas Kolaras discusses how moving to a midsize company creates new opportunities for sharpened thinking and leadership.

title: AI data becomes sticking point in biopharma dealmaking
snippet: As drug hunting gets more complex, where and how data is generated in partnerships between Big Pharmas and AI biotechs is becoming a headache for dealmakers.

title: Wainua flop narrows AstraZeneca’s path to $80B goal ahead of high-risk readouts
snippet: With a Phase 3 failure denting the rare disease drug’s prospects, AstraZeneca has little room for error as it plots a path toward its ambitious 2030 sales target.

title: Biotech’s China problem: An issue of national security or economic value for the US?
snippet: Congressional letters sent to the CEOs of Eli Lilly, Pfizer, Merck, BMS and AbbVie this week voicing concerns about the pharmas' clinical trials in China highlight an ongoing discrepancy in how government and industry think about the rise of the Asian country’s biotech industry.

title: BIO 2026: Cell and gene therapy sector must break the mold to thrive
snippet: If cell and gene therapy makers are going to achieve their mission to improve patients’ lives, the industry must come together to share information across stakeholders, from regulators to manufacturers to payers.

title: At FDA, ‘caretaker mode’ is better than power trip mode
snippet: UniQure’s planned third-quarter submission for its Huntington’s disease gene therapy may be a harbinger of a more flexible FDA under acting commissioner Kyle Diamantas—but how long will it last? And how can companies be sure these positive decisions won’t just be reversed?

title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.
